channel.title: France 24 - International breaking news, top stories and headlines
channel.description: Breaking news and world news from France 24 on Business, Sports, Culture. Video news. News from the US, Europe, Asia Pacific, Africa, Middle East, America

item.title: Ukrainian attacks prompt Russian-held Crimea to halt civilian gasoline sales
item.description: Ukraine has intensified its drone attacks on the Russian-controlled territory of Crimea, prompting a fuel shortage on the peninsula. The campaign has prompted officials there to halt gasoline sales to the public and has caused a larger threat to Russia's fleet in the Black Sea. FRANCE 24's Monte Francis has the story.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Labour's last chance? Burnham set to replace Starmer as UK faces far-right surge
item.description: Another post-Brexit prime minister bites the dust. Keir Starmer owning up to the obvious, that he's too unpopular to lead the Labour Party into the next general election. After replacing the hard left's Jeremy Corbyn, the same Starmer won a whopping landslide two years ago on the promise of stability after all the chaos of Tory rule. Where did it all go wrong? Can the heavy favorite to replace him win back disgruntled Labour voters who first flocked to the Conservatives and now to the far-right Reform party?
item.dc:creator: François PICARD

item.title: US-Iran peace deal 'won't even come close to the expansive detailed Obama agreement'
item.description: Nadia Massih welcomes Larry Sabato, Director of the University of Virginia Center for Politics. JD Vance is hailing "good progress" on US-Iran peace talks, following President Trump's threats to hit Iran "harder than ever before". Sabato reminds us that this war is "almost universally seen as a fiasco" in the US. Seventy percent of Americans are against the war, which is unprecedented. Sabato also offers a scathing assessment of Trump's ever-changing war strategy: "The president cannot stick to a script, and he cannot stick to a prearranged strategy". And so he warns that "it's going to be a long 60 days!"
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: ‘The dad is useless’: Anger grows over French journalist’s Doku criticism
item.description: When Belgium star Jeremy Doku said he wanted to leave the World Cup to attend the birth of his son, it drew a furious reaction from French sports TV presenter France Pierron, who labelled childbirth “disgusting” and said fathers being there was “completely useless”. Her comments have since provoked outrage on social media and elicited support for Doku from fellow footballers.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: As Cuba's power crisis deepens, Havana residents speak out
item.description: Cubans are in the grips of a deepening power and fuel crisis – exacerbated by a US oil blockade launched earlier this year – that has left the country's poorest struggling to cope. FRANCE 24's exclusive report found that residents in Havana are now turning against their own government.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Afro B celebrates his Ivorian roots on 'Mapouka'
item.description: In this edition of our arts24 music show, Jennifer Ben Brahim chats with British Afrobeats superstar Afro B. He is the brains behind the smash hit "Drogba (Joanna)", one of first Afrobeats songs to break the US charts. He has collaborated over the years with the likes of French Montana and Chris Brown. He is back with a new single called "Mapouka", a love letter to Ivory Coast. It’s the first taste of his upcoming album, "Afro Ivoire".
item.dc:creator: Jennifer BEN BRAHIM

item.title: French court refuses trial on use of toxic insecticide in French West Indies
item.description: A French court has ruled against a trial on the use of a known toxic insecticide in the French West Indies, shutting down the possibility of an investigation in a health scandal that has been ongoing for 20 years. Despite being labelled as possibly carcinogenic in 1979, chlordécone was used between 1972 until 1993 - thanks to a by-law that allowed it to circumvent its ban, introduced in 1990. FRANCE 24's Shirli Sitbon tells us more.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: US hails 'good progress' with Iran after first round of talks concludes
item.description: US Vice President JD Vance has said that a first round of US-Iran talks in Switzerland had laid a good foundation for reaching a final deal on ending the Middle East war that the US and Israel began in February. Vance's comments came after he met with Iran's parliamentary speaker Mohammad Bagher Qalibaf on June 21 and 22. The mediation effort in Switzerland had rocky moments, but the talks also led to some agreements between the two sides.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: How can France adapt to recurrent heatwaves?
item.description: France is seeing a second consecutive heatwave in less than a month. Half the country is now under red alert as temperatures are expected to rise to 43°C in the southwestern city of Bordeaux and 39°C in the capital Paris. According to weather service Méteo-France, out of 51 heatwaves recorded nationwide since 1947, two-thirds have occurred since 2000 and about half of those episodes in the last 15 years.
item.dc:creator: Valérie DEKIMPE

item.title: A look back at Starmer's two years in power following his resignation
item.description: Britain's Keir Starmer has announced he's stepping down. The prime minister forced to resign after last month's loses in local elections. Starmer is expected to stay in the role until a new Labour party leader is picked. He is now set to become the country's shortest-serving Labour PM in history.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: France aims for second win in World Cup group stage
item.description: France is playing Iraq on June 22 for the second round of the groupe stage of the 2026 World Cup. The game is taking place in Philadelphia, and 'Les Bleus' will be looking to get a second win after their victory against Senegal. FRANCE 24's Selina Sykes tells us more from Philadelphia's iconic Rocky statue.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Burnham confirms Starmer succession bid as he is sworn in as MP
item.description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer resigned on June 22, forced out by his own party after missteps and mistakes soured voters’ goodwill following a landslide election in 2024. His likely successor, Andy Burnham, has just been sworn in as an MP and has no official rival to run for party leader. FRANCE 24's Philip Turle takes a look.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: France tries to cope with exceptionally high temperatures
item.description: Over half of France has been placed on red alert due to the ferocious heatwave boiling the country. With the temperature reaching up to 41C in some areas, some French cities have cancelled events programmed for the annual Fete de la Musique - Music Celebration. Authorities have also banned alcohol consumption on the streets and asked local event organisers to adapt festivities.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: The 2026 World Cup, France's most successful football manager's swan song
item.description: He is France's most successful football manager, and is looking to win a second World Cup with his team for his last year as the French national coach. But who is Didier Deschamps? FRANCE 24's Aurore Cloé Dupuis went to ask Deschamps' friends and former coach.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Europe under red alert amid exceptional early heatwave
item.description: It's been less than a month since a May heatwave set records across several European countries. Now the heat is back and temperatures across the continent may raise even higher in coming days. According to the WHO, 200,000 people across the EU have died due to heat since 2022.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: French heatwave drives up demand for AC, despite concerns
item.description: The second early-season heatwave of the year has brought the return of a politically-charged issue. That of air-conditioning - for decades, France has been notoriously reticent to adopt the technology - currently just 25% of French households have it. That figure is up significantly from just a decade ago, and it's set to keep rising as climate change brings increasingly frequent episodes of extreme heat. France's largely nuclear-based power generation means that the increased electricity consumption of AC has less of an environmental impact than elsewhere - but there are other concerns.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Who is Andy Burnham's, the UK's likely next prime minister?
item.description: Britain's prime minister is expected to lay out a timetable for his departure. Keir Starmer has been under pressure to go since last month's dismal election outcome for the Labour Party. The man vying the country's top job is the Mayor of Greater Manchester, who's just secured his return to parliament. But who exactly is Andy Burnham?
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: 'Ballet brings me so much joy and I want to spread it': Kadeem En Pointe
item.description: A male ballet dancer is on a mission to encourage more male dancers to take up the more traditional female role of one who goes up on his or her toes, or en pointe. Kadeem En Pointe has filmed himself for the first time here in Paris dancing in front of the city's major monuments. He spoke to us about his ongoing campaign in Perspective.
item.dc:creator: Stuart NORVAL

item.title: For Iraqi minorities, World Cup representation is a source of pride
item.description: France faces Iraq in a group stage match on June 22. The Iraqi team faces a significant disadvantage in this challenging group. But even after a 4-1 loss to Norway in its first match, for the country, this World Cup appearance remains an event not to be missed following a 40 year absence from the competition. This is especially true in Kurdistan in the north of the country where the presence of players from national minorities is significant.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: French government holds crisis meeting over sweltering heatwave
item.description: France is set to experience the hottest day yet in its latest heatwave after a sweltering night, with a peak of 43C expected in the southwest. Forecasters warn the extreme weather episode could end up being as serious as a 2003 heatwave that claimed the lives of nearly 15,000 people nationwide. FRANCE 24's Andrew Hilliar takes a look at the French government's recommendations as it holds its latest crisis meeting.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Trump-backed candidate wins Colombian election
item.description: A fiercely divided Colombia has chosen a new president in a razor-thin runoff that’s already sparking tension across the country. While some celebrate the victory of right-wing candidate Abelardo de la Espriella, others are protesting what they see as a dangerous shift to the right.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: 'JD Vance put lipstick on a pig: Iranians have the upper hand ' in peace negotiations
item.description: Annette Young is pleased to welcome Scott Lucas, Professor of American Studies and Int'l Politics at the Clinton Institute, University College Dublin. According to Lucas, the US is not negotiating with Iran from a position of strength: "The Americans shifted from regime change to regime surrender", he explains, "trying to bomb their way to get the Iranians to capitulate on a number of conditions. That failed." So as the US ruled out ground troops, they had to come "back to the table". And Lucas said the US really miscalculated with respect to: Iran's retaliation on the Gulf states and the impact the blockade of the Strait of Hormuz would have on the global economy.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: What do Iranians think of the Iran-US deal?
item.description: As talks continue between the US and Iran, our colleagues at France 2 got access to rare on-the-ground perspectives in Tehran. Anti-regime residents had hoped that the war would bring change. But with Iranian leaders seemingly more unified than ever, their disappointment is hitting hard.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Maternity leave in India offers progress, with mixed results
item.description: In 2025, the Supreme Court of India declared maternity leave a fundamental right for working women. India is among the countries in the world where pregnant women are entitled to 26 weeks of paid maternity leave, which can be taken for up to two children. The move has been widely welcomed by women's rights advocates. But they emphasise that India still has a long way to go in ensuring the rights of working mothers. FRANCE 24's Navodita Kumari and Zubair Dar report.
item.dc:creator: Navodita KUMARI
